lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  calc
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
      rw [ComplexRMK.variation_rieszMeasure]
      <;> simp [ContinuousLinearMap.isCoeToFun]
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      apply cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
      have h₁ : ‖x‖ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ := by
        -- Use the fact that the norm of a vector is less than or equal to its enorm.
        -- This is because the enorm is defined as the square root of the sum of the squares of the components.
        -- For any vector x, ‖x‖ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ.
        simpa [‖x‖] using Real.le_sqrt_of_sq_le (by
          -- Prove that the square of the norm is less than or equal to the sum of the squares of the components.
          -- This is a direct consequence of the definition of the norm and the enorm.
          simp [‖x‖]
          <;>
          nlinarith [Real.sqrt_nonneg (‖x‖ ^ 2), Real.sq_sqrt (by positivity : (0 : ℝ) ≤ ‖x‖ ^ 2)]
          )
      have h₂ : ‖y‖ ≤ ‖y‖ₑ := by
        -- Similarly, ‖y‖ ≤ ‖y‖ₑ.
        simpa [‖y‖] using Real.le_sqrt_of_sq_le (by
          simp [‖y‖]
          <;>
          nlinarith [Real.sqrt_nonneg (‖y‖ ^ 2), Real.sq_sqrt (by positivity : (0 : ℝ) ≤ ‖y‖ ^ 2)]
          )
      -- Since both ‖x‖ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ and ‖y‖ ≤ ‖y‖ₑ, we can multiply these inequalities.
      -- This gives ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ, which is the desired result.
      calc
        ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ := by
          gcongr
        _ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
          gcongr

have h_main : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
    -- Use the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani representation theorem to relate the variation to the operator norm
    have h₁ : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
      -- The variation of the measure is bounded by the norm of the linear functional
      exact ComplexRMK.estimate_rieszMeasure (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
    -- Use the previously established bound on the norm of the linear functional
    have h₂ : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      apply cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound
    -- Combine the inequalities
    calc
      (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := h₁
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := h₂
  
  have h_final : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
    simpa [norm_eq_norm] using h_main
  
  exact h_final

have h₁ : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
    -- Use the fact that the variation of the measure is bounded by the norm of the linear functional
    calc
      (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ = (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).total_variation := by simp [ComplexAvg.innerMeasure]
      _ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
        -- Apply the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem to get the bound
        have h₂ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).innerMeasure‖ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
          -- The norm of the inner measure is the norm of the linear functional
          simp [ComplexAvg.rieszMeasure, ComplexLinearMap.norm_eq]
        have h₃ : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).total_variation ≤ ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).innerMeasure‖ := by
          -- The total variation is bounded by the norm of the inner measure
          calc
            (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).total_variation = (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).total_variation := rfl
            _ ≤ ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).innerMeasure‖ := by
              -- Use the property of the total variation and the inner measure
              have h₄ : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).total_variation = (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).total_variation := rfl
              have h₅ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).innerMeasure‖ = ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).innerMeasure‖ := rfl
              -- Use the fact that the total variation is less than or equal to the norm of the inner measure
              exact ComplexAverageMeasure.total_variation_le_norm ha x y
        -- Combine the inequalities to get the final result
        calc
          (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).total_variation ≤ ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).innerMeasure‖ := h₃
          _ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by rw [h₂]
  have h₂ : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
    -- Use the previously established bound on the linear functional
    calc
      ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
        apply cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by rfl
  have h₃ : (‖x‖ : ℝ) * ‖y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
    -- Use the fact that the norm of x is less than or equal to its extended norm

    have h₅ : (‖y‖ : ℝ) ≤ ‖y‖ₑ := by

    -- Multiply the inequalities to get the final result

        exact mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_right h₄ (norm_nonneg y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
        exact mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left h₅ (norm_nonneg x)
  -- Combine the inequalities to get the final result
  calc
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := h₁
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := h₂
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := h₃

have h₁ : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
    -- Prove that the total variation of the measure is equal to the norm of the linear functional
    have h₂ : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
      -- Use the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem to establish the equality

        -- Use the fact that the total variation of the measure is the norm of the linear functional

      -- The above step uses the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem to prove the equality
      exact h₃
    -- The result follows directly from the equality established above
    exact h₂
  
  have h₂ : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
    -- This is a direct application of the previous lemma

      _ = ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by rw [h₃]
  exact h₄

have h₁ : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
  have h₂ : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
    -- Use the fact that the variation of the Riesz measure is bounded by the norm of the linear functional
    have h₃ : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) = ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap) := rfl
    rw [h₃]
    -- Use the property of the Riesz measure to bound the variation by the norm of the functional
    have h₄ : (ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
      -- This follows from the fact that the norm of the functional is the total variation of the measure
      have h₅ : (ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)).variation Set.univ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
        -- This is a property of the Riesz measure: its total variation is the norm of the functional
        have h₆ : (ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)).variation Set.univ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
          -- This is a direct application of the Riesz-Markov theorem
          simp [ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure, ContinuousLinearMap.norm_def, MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace.measurability, Real.norm_eq_abs]
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try simp_all [Complex.norm_eq_abs, Complex.abs, Complex.normSq, Real.sqrt_le_iff, sq_nonneg, add_nonneg, mul_self_nonneg]) <;>
          (try ring_nf) <;>
          (try norm_cast) <;>
          (try field_simp [Real.sqrt_eq_iff_sq_eq]) <;>
          (try linarith) <;>
          (try nlinarith)
          <;>
          simp_all [norm_eq_abs, Complex.abs, Complex.normSq, Real.sqrt_le_iff, sq_nonneg, add_nonneg, mul_self_nonneg]
          <;>
          ring_nf at *
          <;>
          norm_num at *
          <;>
          linarith
        exact h₆
      rw [h₅]
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try simp_all [Complex.norm_eq_abs, Complex.abs, Complex.normSq, Real.sqrt_le_iff, sq_nonneg, add_nonneg, mul_self_nonneg]) <;>
      (try ring_nf) <;>
      (try norm_cast) <;>
      (try field_simp [Real.sqrt_eq_iff_sq_eq]) <;>
      (try linarith) <;>
      (try nlinarith)
      <;>
      simp_all [norm_eq_abs, Complex.abs, Complex.normSq, Real.sqrt_le_iff, sq_nonneg, add_nonneg, mul_self_nonneg]
      <;>
      ring_nf at *
      <;>
      norm_num at *
      <;>
      linarith
    exact h₄
  calc
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := h₂
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := h₁
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
      -- Since the Euclidean norm in a Hilbert space is the same as the norm of the space, this step is trivial
      simp [norm_eq_abs, Real.norm_eq_abs, abs_of_nonneg, norm_nonneg, mul_comm]
      <;>
      norm_cast
      <;>
      simp_all [Complex.abs, Complex.normSq, mul_comm, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm,
        Real.sqrt_le_sqrt_iff, sq_nonneg, add_nonneg, mul_self_nonneg]
      <;>
      nlinarith [sq_nonneg (‖x‖ - ‖y‖)]
